position_information.desired_rate_of_pay: $23.00 per hour (negotiable based on experience and responsibilities)
position_information.how_did_you_learn_about_position: SWCAP website and referral from current employee (Angela Peterson, Family Services Manager)
position_information.computer_software_tools_equipment:
Proficient in Microsoft Office Suite (Word, Excel, PowerPoint, Outlook) for case notes, data tracking, mail merges, and presentation preparation; experienced with Google Workspace (Docs, Sheets, Drive, Calendar) for collaborative work and shared documentation; regular user of Zoom and Microsoft Teams for virtual client meetings, inter-agency collaboration, and remote trainings. Comfortable with client database and case management systems such as ClientTrack and similar web-based platforms for intake, eligibility determination, service planning, and documentation. Familiar with electronic health/behavioral health record systems from prior work with a counseling agency (used for documenting sessions, uploading forms, and generating reports). Able to efficiently use web-based benefits application portals (e.g., ACCESS for Wisconsin benefits) to help clients apply for and manage services.

On the equipment side, comfortable using standard office equipment including multi-line phone systems, scanners, copiers, fax machines, and postage meters. Experienced in using laptops, projectors, and basic A/V equipment for community presentations and workshops. Regularly uses smartphones and tablets to access calendars, email, and secure messaging while in the field. Typing speed approximately 55–60 words per minute with strong attention to spelling and grammar when entering case notes or drafting professional correspondence.
